message:
fix(spawn): use rig-level beads instead of mayor/rig path

The spawn command and polecat manager were incorrectly looking for beads
in <rig>/mayor/rig/.beads instead of <rig>/.beads. This caused:
- `gt spawn --issue ga-xxp` to fail with "no issue found" even when
  `bd show ga-xxp` worked from the rig directory
- Polecats couldn't find rig-level issues (with ga- prefix) because
  mayor/rig has a different beads database (with gt- prefix)

This fix:
- spawn.go: Use r.Path instead of filepath.Join(r.Path, "mayor", "rig")
- polecat/manager.go: Use r.Path for beads.New() initialization
- session/manager.go: Use r.Path/.beads for BEADS_DIR environment

Closes beads-2nh

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"gt-69l","title":"Hook system for event extensibility","description":"GGT needs hook system for extensibility like PGT.

## Event Types
```go
type Event string
const (
    EventPreSessionStart  Event = "pre-session-start"
    EventPostSessionStart Event = "post-session-start"
    EventPreShutdown      Event = "pre-shutdown"
    EventPostShutdown     Event = "post-shutdown"
    EventOnPaneOutput     Event = "on-pane-output"
    EventSessionIdle      Event = "session-idle"
    EventMailReceived     Event = "mail-received"
    EventWorkAssigned     Event = "work-assigned"
)
```

## Hook Configuration
File: .claude/hooks.json or .gastown/hooks.json
```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "pre-shutdown": [
      {"type": "command", "cmd": "./scripts/pre-shutdown.sh"}
    ],
    "on-pane-output": [
      {"type": "command", "cmd": "./scripts/activity-monitor.sh"}
    ]
  }
}
```

## Hook Types
1. **Command**: Execute external script
2. **Built-in**: Internal Go functions (pre-shutdown checks)

## Hook Interface
```go
type HookRunner struct {
    config *HookConfig
}

type HookResult struct {
    Success bool
    Message string
    Block   bool  // For pre-* hooks: should operation be blocked?
}

func (r *HookRunner) Fire(event Event, ctx *HookContext) []HookResult
```

## CLI Commands
```
gt hooks list [<event>]    # List registered hooks
gt hooks fire <event>      # Manually fire for testing
gt hooks test [--all]      # Validate hook config
```

## Integration Points
- internal/session/manager.go: Fire pre/post session hooks
- internal/mail/router.go: Fire mail-received hook

## New Package
internal/hooks/
├── types.go    # Event, HookConfig, HookResult
├── runner.go   # HookRunner, Fire()
└── builtin.go  # Built-in hooks (pre-shutdown checks)

## PGT Reference
gastown-py/src/gastown/hooks/

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Hook config loading from JSON
- [ ] Command hooks execute subprocess
- [ ] Pre-shutdown hook integration with session stop
- [ ] CLI for listing and testing hooks","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:47:34.584907-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:04:47.890588-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-6k8","title":"Interrupt vs Queue mail semantics","description":"Add priority/delivery semantics to mail messages.

## Semantics

| Type | Delivery | Use Case |
|------|----------|----------|
| Interrupt | tmux send-keys | Lifecycle, URGENT, stuck detection |
| Queue | Create message only | Normal mail, status, heartbeat |

## Implementation

- `bd mail send --interrupt` uses tmux send-keys notification
- Default is queue (agent checks with `gt mail check`)
- Urgent flag on messages for interrupt delivery

## Agent Side

- `gt mail check --quiet` - non-blocking check for queued mail
- `gt mail wait` - block until mail arrives (for idle agents)
- Heartbeats become queued, agent checks at natural breakpoints","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:28.408196-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T17:22:52.555329-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T16:31:00.450061-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-6k8","depends_on_id":"gt-99m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:46.529252-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-8j8e","title":"gt mail send: --priority flag should work like bd mail send","description":"UX inconsistency: gt mail send passes flags that bd mail send doesn't support.

## Root Cause

router.go line 42 passes `--priority` to bd:
```go
args = append(args, "--priority", fmt.Sprintf("%d", beadsPriority))
```

But `bd mail send` only has `--urgent` (boolean), not `--priority`.

## Fix Options

1. Add `--priority` flag to `bd mail send` (preferred - more expressive)
2. Change router to only use `--urgent` when priority=0

## Also Affected

- `--type` flag (line 46) - bd mail send doesn't have this
- `--thread-id` flag (line 51) - bd mail send doesn't have this  
- `--reply-to` flag (line 56) - bd mail send doesn't have this

The router assumes bd mail send has features it doesn't have.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-18T21:31:05.486487-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T12:07:28.918346-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T12:07:28.918346-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-8mbz","title":"Town Doctor molecule for harness health checks","description":"Create a Town Doctor molecule that any Gas Town agent can run to diagnose and repair harness issues.

## Concept

Instead of just `gt doctor` as a CLI command with hardcoded checks, create a **molecule** (checklist workflow) that:
- Any agent (Mayor, Witness, Polecat) can instantiate
- Walks the agent through diagnostic steps
- Agent uses judgment to fix issues found
- Works as a structured troubleshooting guide

## Why a Molecule?

1. **Agent-driven**: The agent running it becomes "the doctor" temporarily
2. **Extensible**: Add new checks by updating the molecule, not code
3. **Contextual**: Agent can reason about issues, not just run scripts
4. **Self-healing**: Agent can fix problems it finds, not just report them

## Proposed Checks (molecule steps)

1. Verify harness structure (mayor/, .beads/, CLAUDE.md exist)
2. Validate config files (town.json, rigs.json parse correctly)
3. Check beads health (bd doctor, redirect validity)
4. Verify git state (clean working tree, proper remotes)
5. Check rig integrity (each registered rig exists, has config.json)
6. Validate agent clones (mayor/rig/, refinery/rig/ exist and are valid)
7. Check for orphaned worktrees/branches
8. Verify daemon state (if running)

## Integration

- `gt doctor` could instantiate the molecule for the current agent
- Or agent can run `bd ready` and pick up doctor tasks when prioritized
- Results logged to beads for audit trail

## Related

- gt-cr9: Harness Design & Documentation (completed)
- Molecules design in architecture.md","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-19T13:03:22.688851-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T13:03:22.688851-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-ov2","title":"Refinery agent: merge queue processing loop","description":"The Refinery agent processes the merge queue, merging polecat work to main.

## Interface

```go
type Refinery struct {
    rig    *Rig
    queue  *MergeQueue
    git    *Git
    mail   *Mailbox
    config RefineryConfig
}

type RefineryConfig struct {
    AutoMerge     bool          // Auto-merge passing MRs
    RunTests      bool          // Run tests before merge
    TestCommand   string        // Command to run tests
    RequireReview bool          // Require review before merge (future)
}

func NewRefinery(rig *Rig, ...) *Refinery

// Lifecycle
func (r *Refinery) Start() error
func (r *Refinery) Stop() error
func (r *Refinery) IsRunning() bool

// Processing
func (r *Refinery) ProcessQueue() error
func (r *Refinery) ProcessMR(mr *MergeRequest) error
```

## Processing Loop

1. Check queue for pending MRs
2. For each pending MR:
   a. Fetch polecat branch
   b. Attempt merge to refinery/rig (local main)
   c. Run tests if configured
   d. If pass: push to origin, mark merged
   e. If fail: mark rejected, notify polecat
3. Sleep, repeat

## Merge Strategy

- Fast-forward when possible
- Merge commit when not
- On conflict: reject MR, polecat must rebase

## Test Integration

If tests configured:
```bash
cd refinery/rig
git merge polecat/branch
<test_command>  # e.g., go test ./...
```
Result determines merge/reject.

## Notifications

On merge success:
- Mail to polecat: "Your work merged"
- Update bead if issue tracked

On merge failure:
- Mail to polecat: "Merge failed: <reason>"
- Include conflict details if applicable

## Dependencies

Needs: Rig management, Git wrapper, Mail system, Merge queue","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:22:08.498771-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T14:20:35.032221-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T14:20:35.032221-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-ov2","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:22:21.801826-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-ov2","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:22:21.89716-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-q8du","title":"Configuration in Beads: use pinned beads for rig/agent config","description":"## Summary

Move configuration from JSON files into Beads data plane using pinned beads.

## Motivation

- Config becomes versionable and syncable like issues
- Agents can read config via `bd show`
- Unified data model for everything
- Supports the 'Beads as Universal Data Plane' vision (gt-aqm)

## Config Types to Migrate

### Rig-level (pinned per rig)
- Theme/colors for tmux status line
- Default branch naming conventions
- Merge queue settings
- Witness thresholds

### Agent-level (pinned per role)
- Handoffs (already planned - gt-cu7r)
- Agent preferences
- Current focus/context

### Town-level (pinned at town root)
- Cross-rig settings
- Federation config
- Global themes

## Implementation

1. Implement StatusPinned (beads-6v2) ✓ in progress
2. Create config pinned beads on rig init
3. Add `bd config get/set` commands that read/write pinned beads
4. Migrate existing config.json fields

## Naming Convention

```
<prefix>-cfg-<scope>
gt-cfg-theme      # gastown theme
bd-cfg-merge      # beads merge settings
gm-cfg-town       # town-level config
```

## Dependencies

- beads-6v2: Add StatusPinned to beads schema
- gm-w13: Pinned Beads epic","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-18T21:58:59.898116-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T21:58:59.898116-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-qao","title":"CLI: mayor commands (start, attach, stop, status)","description":"Mayor management CLI commands.

## Commands

### gt mayor start
```
gt mayor start [--continue] [--agent AGENT]
```
- --continue: Resume from previous session (check for handoff mail)
- --agent: claude (default) or other agent type

### gt mayor attach
```
gt mayor attach
```
Attach to running Mayor session.

### gt mayor stop
```
gt mayor stop [--grace-period N]
```
Stop Mayor session with optional grace period.

### gt mayor status
```
gt mayor status [--json]
```
Show Mayor running status.

## Session Management
Session name: `gt-mayor`
Working directory: `<town>/mayor/rig/` or `<town>/mayor/`

## Implementation
Similar to session commands but for special Mayor context.

```go
func getMayorPath(townRoot string) string {
    return filepath.Join(townRoot, "mayor")
}

func getMayorSessionName() string {
    return "gt-mayor"
}
```

## New File
internal/cmd/mayor.go

## PGT Reference
gastown-py/src/gastown/cli/mayor_cmd.py

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] gt mayor start launches Mayor session
- [ ] gt mayor attach works
- [ ] gt mayor stop with grace period
- [ ] gt mayor status shows running state","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:47:54.721035-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:05:45.226324-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-qh2","title":"Session cycling UX: smooth transitions via TUI wrapper","description":"## Problem

Current CLI agent session cycling is painful:
- Shell → CC starts → priming → context loads → ready → work → exit/crash → repeat
- Each cycle is 30-60 seconds of cold boot
- No continuity between shell and agent's inner state
- Raw "session not running, starting..." loop is the baseline

## GGT Advantages (already have)

- Beads: Work state survives session death completely
- Mail: Handoff notes from past-self to future-self  
- Prime commands: Structured context reload

## Gap: Transition Mechanics

Ideas to explore when actively using CLI:

1. **In-band cycling** - `/restart` or `/cycle` command, agent handles own restart without dropping to shell

2. **Hot standby** - TUI maintains pre-warmed session in background, switch to already-primed agent

3. **Persistent wrapper** - Bubbletea TUI stays running across session cycles, CC sessions come/go inside it

4. **Session pooling** - Keep 2-3 primed sessions ready, never wait for cold start

## Deferred

Deliberately P4 until we're actively using the simpler CLI and feel the pain firsthand.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T20:38:12.660716-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T23:17:34.27061-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-u82","title":"Design: Mayor session cycling and handoff","description":"Design for Mayor session cycling and structured handoff.

## Overview

Mayor coordinates across all rigs and runs for extended periods. Needs session cycling pattern with structured handoff notes.

## Key Elements

1. Session cycling recognition (when to cycle)
2. Handoff note format (structured state capture)

- gt-sye: Mayor startup protocol prompting

 {"id":"gt-v5k","title":"Design: Failure modes and recovery","description":"Document failure modes and recovery strategies for Gas Town operations.

## Critical Failure Modes

### 1. Agent Crash Mid-Operation

**Scenario**: Polecat crashes while committing, Witness crashes while verifying

**Detection**:
- Session suddenly gone (tmux check fails)
- State shows 'working' but no session
- Heartbeat stops (for Witness)

**Recovery**:
- Doctor detects via ZombieSessionCheck
- Capture any recoverable state
- Reset agent state to 'idle'
- For Witness: auto-restart via supervisor or manual gt witness start

### 2. Git State Corruption

**Scenario**: Merge conflict, failed rebase, detached HEAD

**Detection**:
- Git commands fail
- Dirty state that won't commit
- Branch diverged from origin

**Recovery**:
- gt doctor reports git health issues
- Manual intervention recommended
- Severe cases: remove clone, re-clone

### 3. Beads Sync Conflict

**Scenario**: Two polecats modify same issue

**Detection**:
- bd sync fails with conflict
- Beads tombstone mechanism handles most cases

**Recovery**:
- Beads has last-write-wins semantics
- bd sync --force in extreme cases
- Issues may need manual dedup

### 4. Tmux Failure

**Scenario**: Tmux server crashes, socket issues

**Detection**:
- All sessions inaccessible
- "no server running" errors

**Recovery**:
- Kill any orphan processes
- tmux kill-server && tmux start-server
- All agent states reset to idle
- Re-spawn active work

### 5. Claude API Issues

**Scenario**: Rate limits, outages, context limits

**Detection**:
- Sessions hang or produce errors
- Repeated failure patterns

**Recovery**:
- Exponential backoff (handled by Claude Code)
- For context limits: session cycling (mail-to-self)
- For outages: wait and retry

### 6. Disk Full

**Scenario**: Clones, logs, or beads fill disk

**Detection**:
- Write operations fail
- git/bd commands error

**Recovery**:
- Clean up logs: rm ~/.gastown/logs/*
- Remove old polecat clones
- gt doctor --fix can clean some cruft

### 7. Network Failure

**Scenario**: Can't reach GitHub, API servers

**Detection**:
- git fetch/push fails
- Claude sessions hang

**Recovery**:
- Work continues locally
- Queue pushes for later
- Sync when connectivity restored

## Recovery Principles

1. **Fail safe**: Prefer stopping over corrupting
2. **State is recoverable**: Git and beads have recovery mechanisms
3. **Doctor heals**: gt doctor --fix handles common issues
4. **Emergency stop**: gt stop --all as last resort
5. **Human escalation**: Some failures need Overseer intervention

## Implementation

- Document each failure mode in architecture.md
- Ensure doctor checks cover detection
- Add recovery hints to error messages
- Log all failures for debugging","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:19:07.198289-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T23:19:28.171942-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-vc1n","title":"Tmux status line: rig color themes and worker identity display","description":"## Summary

Customize tmux status line for Gas Town workers with:
1. Per-rig configurable color themes
2. Clear worker name and role visibility

## Current Problem

- Only mayor shows in status line (and truncated)
- Can't tell which rig/worker you're looking at
- All sessions look the same

## Proposed Design

### Per-rig colors
```yaml
# In rig config or beads
theme:
  primary: '#ff6600'    # Orange for gastown
  secondary: '#333333'
  accent: '#ffcc00'
```

### Status line format
```
[gastown/Rictus] polecat | gt-70b3 | branch: polecat/Rictus
[beads/emma] crew | working | branch: main  
[mayor] coordinator | idle
```

### Components
- Rig name with rig color
- Worker name
- Role (polecat/crew/mayor/witness/refinery)
- Current issue or status
- Branch name

## Configuration

Could use pinned beads for this (see gm-w13, beads-6v2):
- `bd show <rig>-theme` returns theme config
- Stored as pinned bead, always available
- Part of 'config in beads data plane' initiative","notes":"Implementation complete. Core features: per-rig color themes, dynamic status line with issue/mail indicators, gt theme/issue commands. Ready for testing.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-18T21:58:58.547188-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T14:25:02.135073-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-vci","title":"Mayor handoff mail template","description":"Add MAYOR_HANDOFF mail template to templates.py.

## Template Function

def mayor_handoff(
    active_swarms: List[SwarmStatus],
    rig_status: Dict[str, RigStatus],
    pending_escalations: List[Escalation],
    in_flight_decisions: List[Decision],
    recent_actions: List[str],
    delegated_work: List[DelegatedItem],
    user_requests: List[str],
    next_steps: List[str],
    warnings: Optional[str] = None,
    session_duration: Optional[str] = None,
) -> Message:
    metadata = {
        'template': 'MAYOR_HANDOFF',
        'timestamp': datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
        'session_duration': session_duration,
        'active_swarm_count': len(active_swarms),
        'pending_escalation_count': len(pending_escalations),
    }
    # ... format sections ...
    return Message.create(
        sender='mayor/',
        recipient='mayor/',
        subject='Session Handoff',
        body=body,
        priority='high',
    )

## Metadata Fields

- template: MAYOR_HANDOFF
- timestamp: ISO format
- session_duration: Human readable
- active_swarm_count: Number of active swarms
- pending_escalation_count: Number of escalations

## Mail Priority

Use priority='high' to ensure handoff is seen on startup.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T20:15:30.26323-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T20:48:59.550689-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-vci","depends_on_id":"gt-u82","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T20:15:39.554108-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-w9o","title":"/restart: Personal slash command for in-place agent restart","description":"Create ~/.claude/commands/restart.md that restarts current Gas Town agent in place.

## Detection
- Read tmux session name: gt-mayor, gt-witness-*, gt-refinery-*, gt-polecat-*
- Fallback: check GT_ROLE env var

## Behavior by role
- mayor: gt mayor restart (sends Ctrl-C, loop respawns)
- witness: gt witness restart
- refinery: gt refinery restart  
- polecat: gt polecat restart (or witness-mediated)

## Command format
Uses backticks for inline bash to detect context, then instructs Claude to run appropriate restart.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:32:30.043125-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T18:43:17.182303-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-18T18:43:17.182303-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-wpg","title":"Replaceable notifications via Claude Code queue","description":"Leverage Claude Code's ability to replace queued text for notifications that supersede previous ones.

## Problem

If daemon sends 10 heartbeats while agent is busy, agent returns to see 10 stacked messages. Wasteful and noisy.

## Solution

Use Claude Code's queue replacement for:
- Heartbeat messages (only latest matters)
- Status updates that supersede previous
- Progress notifications

## Implementation

Notifications get a 'slot' identifier. New notification in same slot replaces old one:
- Slot: 'heartbeat' → only one heartbeat queued at a time
- Slot: 'status-<rig>' → latest status per rig
- No slot → stacks normally (for unique messages)

## Research Needed

- How does Claude Code expose queue replacement?
- tmux send-keys behavior with pending input
- Alternative: clear + resend pattern","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:29.821949-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:29.821949-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-wpg","depends_on_id":"gt-99m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:46.656972-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
